method: offset-decoy: peak presence at a compound's real adduct m/z vs nulls; anchor-swap is the fair null
adduct_panel: the 5 mechanisms actually assigned in the demo (verified vs ionization_mechanism table): pos +H+/+NH4+/+(CH4N2O)H+, neg -H+/+Br-. Earlier runs wrongly included +HBrBr- (not a real mechanism) and +CO3- (defined but never assigned).
recommendation: fold as additive per-adduct log-odds on logit(p_correct), capped (<=+3) and shrunk; weights are instrument/library-specific -> calibration store (D6); monoisotopic-only => conservative lower bound
caveats: weights encode Br/Ur reagent chemistry; do not hardcode universally
'present' is monoisotopic-only, so LRs are a conservative lower bound vs a fit-gated corroboration
anchor-swap pool includes real primaries -> also conservative
+Br2-/+Br3-/+NO3-/+CO3- are defined in the library but not assigned in this dataset; if the target collection lacks ion rows for them they'd be untapped corroboration channels (unverified)
